contentBlocks.original:
ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय धन्वन्तरये अमृतकलश हस्ताय
सर्वामय विनाशनाय त्रैलोक्यनाथाय श्री महाविष्णवे नमः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya dhanvantaraye amṛtakalaśa hastāya
sarvāmaya vināśanāya trailokyanāthāya śrī mahāviṣṇave namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Lord Dhanvantari, the divine healer with the pot of nectar, who removes all afflictions and diseases. I bow to Lord Vishnu, the sustainer of the three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred sound; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; भगवते (bhagavate) – to the blessed one; वासुदेवाय (vāsudevāya) – to Vāsudeva; धन्वन्तरये (dhanvantaraye) – to Dhanvantari; अमृतकलश (amṛtakalaśa) – nectar pot (amṛta:nectar | kalaśa:pot); हस्ताय (hastāya) – to the hand; सर्वामय (sarvāmaya) – all diseases (sarva:all | āmaya:diseases); विनाशनाय (vināśanāya) – for destruction; त्रैलोक्यनाथाय (trailokyanāthāya) – to the lord of three worlds (trai:three | lokya:worlds | nāthāya:to the lord); श्री (śrī) – auspicious; महाविष्णवे (mahāviṣṇave) – to the great Vishnu (mahā:great | viṣṇave:to Vishnu); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;
